name: Three Questions for the Node Below
description: Serel has three questions for Arlen: has the Terran node felt the Aetherian node, has the connection changed in the last eight months, and does Arlen understand what Seraphina is trying to accomplish. She also has one thing to tell him separately.
accept_text: She writes it out — the three questions in Aetherian notation, translating as she writes. Three precise lines. 'One: has the Terran node been aware of the Seraphina node during its seventeen years of operation. Two: has the quality of that awareness changed in the last eight months. Three: does the Terran node understand what the Seraphina node is trying to accomplish.' She folds the paper and holds it out. 'I know that asking a Terran soldier to carry a question from an Aetherian commander to a crystal-integrated man in a Grid node is a specific kind of errand,' she says. The dry quality in her voice is present. 'But you are the only person currently operating in both contexts simultaneously, and the question is urgent enough that I'm setting the cross-faction part aside.' She pauses. 'Tell Arlen that Serel of Fort Sky-Watch sends the question. Kira will have told him who I am.' She looks at the pass. 'And tell him one more thing — separately from the three questions. Tell him: fifteen years ago, the frequency at the Seraphina altar felt like someone who had been waiting a long time to say something. Tell him that exactly. He will understand it better than I can from outside the lattice.'
complete_text: Arlen is quiet after you deliver the three questions and Serel's separate note. The crystal holds a sustained brightness — the warmth of something processing something large. 'Yes,' he says. 'The answer to all three is yes.' He is quiet. 'I have felt the Seraphina node since the beginning of my seventeen years in the lattice. It has always been there — dimmer than the primary nodes, quieter, at lower intensity. I assumed it was a dormant node, something running on passive cycle without a mind occupying it.' The brightness steadies. 'In the last eight months it has brightened. Not dramatically, but consistently and directionally — the same directionality as the Grid's overall reactivation. As if it is coming online at the same rate as the rest of the network.' He pauses. 'What I have not been able to determine, from inside the lattice, is whether it is responding to the Grid's reactivation — waking because the network is waking — or whether it has been active for three hundred years and is now simply visible because the connections are closing.' He is quiet. 'Serel's third question: does the Terran node understand what the Seraphina node is trying to accomplish. Tell Serel: I did not know there was a mind in the Seraphina node until this moment. I have been feeling it as signal, not as intention.' A pause. The crystal brightens in a new way — the way of something deciding something. 'Now that I know, I am going to try to reach it directly. Node-to-node communication across that distance. I do not know if it is possible. I have never tried.' He pauses. 'Will you go back to Seraphina? I am going to begin reaching toward it now. I want to know what it says when it feels me reaching. And if it responds — if it reaches back in a way it was not reaching before — tell me what that is like from where you're standing, outside the crystal.'
